stemHtml: Carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning leads to hypoxia because..

options.a: CO is far less easily attached (200 times) to haemoglobin than oxygen.
options.b: accumulation of CO in blood leads to hyperventilation and thus to hypoxia.
options.c: CO in blood displaces oxygen from the blood corpuscles impairing oxygen transport.
options.d: the increasing amount of CO in the lung alveoles gradually reverses oxygen diffusion.
